data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== GitHub cross-check: 2026 critic skills addendum
=== agent abbed9708fb3ff536

The final agent recovered and delivered substantial new material — a whole tier of rigorous 2026 critic skills I hadn't surfaced. This materially strengthens several conclusions and changes one of them. Let me deliver the consolidated addendum.

---

# ADDENDUM — incorporating the 2025–2026 entrants stream

The final research stream recovered and surfaced a tier of purpose-built 2026 critic skills that **changes one conclusion and sharpens three others**. Key new sources with verbatim mechanics:

| New source | Stars / date | Verdict shape | Evidence | Gate | Sep. critic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **flowglad `gameplan-review`** | **1,716★**, 2026-05-17 | 3-value `Approve / Request Changes / Comment` | yes (file paths) | block-with-override | yes |
| **wan-huiyan/agent-review-panel** | 21★, 2026-06-08 | **4-value + 1–10 + confidence + per-finding abstain** | yes (`[UNVERIFIED]` demotion) | advise | yes (adversarial panel) |
| **russelltsherman/qrspi** | small | binary, **fail-CLOSED** | mandatory file+symbol | fail-closed | yes |
| **Tetra-Research/jig `/plan-review`** | small | Approve/Request-Changes/Needs-Discussion | "file and line numbers (Non-Negotiable)" | advise | yes |
| **toderian plan-critic** | small | PROCEED/REVISE/BLOCKED | "unverified claims score 1" | loop-gate | yes |
| **michaelhazza framework** | small | implement/discuss/defer/reject | `source_refs[]` empty ⇒ **finding dropped** | advise | yes |
| **maratik123/quartzite** | small | GO/ITERATE/STOP | yes | evaluator-optimizer loop | yes |
| **johnlindquist `design-doc-gatekeeper`** | 41★ | missing-sections checklist (no score) | template-anchored | advisory Action | yes |
| **Azure-Samples agent-architecture-review** | 85★ (MSFT) | severity buckets | not enforced | advisory MCP | single agent |
| **buildermethods/agent-os** | 4,909★ | v2 ✅/⚠️/❌; **critic REMOVED in v3** | yes | advise | yes (gone) |

## What this CHANGES vs my prior report

**3(a) Three-valued verdict — UPGRADE from "refuted among coding tools" to "actually the dominant shape among purpose-built plan critics."** My first pass only had plan-critique/rfc-reviewer/Spec-Kit and concluded no coding tool ships three-valued. That was wrong on volume. Three-valued is now the **most common** shape in dedicated plan/RFC critics: flowglad `Approve/Request-Changes/Comment`, jig `Approve/Request-Changes/Needs-Discussion`, quartzite `GO/ITERATE/STOP`, toderian `PROCEED/REVISE/BLOCKED`, rfc-reviewer `PROCEED/HOLD/BLOCKED`. **Your PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL is squarely mainstream for this artifact type** — it was only absent from *vendor* plan modes (Cursor/Claude Code), which are human-gated.

**Crucially for your AMBIGUOUS verdict specifically — explicit abstain/uncertain is now corroborated by ≥3 shipping tools, not just the Autorubric research paper:**
- **wan-huiyan/agent-review-panel**: per-finding `[VR_INCONCLUSIVE]` state + confidence levels; "Precise mode demotes uncited findings to `[UNVERIFIED]`."
- **OpenSpec verify**: "prefer SUGGESTION when uncertain" (severity-downgrade-under-uncertainty).
- **qrspi / SmithyCLI**: dedicated low-confidence / `nonBlockingNotes` channel.
- **BMAD**: `n/a` dimensions.
This is the strongest single validation in the whole study: your AMBIGUOUS→advisory mapping is exactly what the rigorous tier does (downgrade-under-uncertainty rather than force PASS/FAIL).

**3(b) Cite-or-omit — UPGRADE from "rare, only BMAD" to "near-universal among the rigorous tier, and two tools implement your exact drop-if-uncited rule."** My first pass undercounted because I'd only seen BMAD. The new data shows your discipline is the *defining* feature of serious 2026 critics:
- **michaelhazza**: JSON `source_refs[]` — "**Empty array means the finding is dropped**." This is literally your cite-or-omit rule.
- **wan-huiyan**: uncited findings demoted to `[UNVERIFIED]` + a claim-verification phase.
- **toderian**: "unverified claims score 1."
- **qrspi**: fail-closed with mandatory file+symbol citation.
- **jig**: "Quality Bar (Non-Negotiable)… file and line numbers."
- **quantumssh CI**: "anchor each finding to a CLAUDE.md rule, threat-model section, or ADR."
Still absent from the high-star persona collections (wshobson 36,958★, VoltAgent 22,091★, Azure-Samples) — confirming the flag that **stars ≠ rigor**. The popular ones are vibes; the rigorous ones are obscure.

**3(e) Separate critic — REINFORCED, plus a notable counter-signal.** Separate-critic-with-explicit-protocol is the norm for purpose-built tools (rfc-reviewer+rfc-starter author/critic split, BMAD rubric-walker, quartzite/toderian/michaelhazza evaluator-optimizer loops, wagner-santos blind multi-LLM peer review). **Counter-signal worth weighing in your design rationale: buildermethods/agent-os v3 REMOVED its v2 `spec-verifier` critic**, betting frontier models self-verify well enough. That's a live bet against your separate-critic premise from a 4,909★ project — worth a sentence in your design doc as the dissenting view (your rebuttal: you're gating fail-closed at claim-time, not running an inline revision loop, so self-verification by the author defeats the purpose).

## What stays UNCHANGED

- **Gap analysis (§1):** no new *dimensions* appeared — the new tools recombine the same axes (clarity, coverage, feasibility, risk, dependencies, evidence). GAP-1 (normative phrasing), GAP-6 (observability), GAP-8 (contract depth) still stand as the real holes. Notably, flowglad's "Agent-Readiness checklist" (unambiguous steps, **file paths specified**, dependencies explicit, success criteria, edge cases) independently corroborates B1+B3+L4 again.
- **3(c) Fail-CLOSED:** still rare and still your distinctive bet. New true-blockers found are the same two flavors — **human gate** (spec-workflow-mcp, "Verbal approval is NEVER accepted," no override) and **mechanical/CI** (OpenSpec `validate --strict`). The one new self-contained *automated* gate is **qrspi's fail-closed binary** (`pass:false ⟺ findings non-empty`) — the closest precedent to your fail-closed intent, and notably it pairs fail-closed with mandatory citation (exactly your sound-check coupling). rfc-reviewer's **score-caps** remain the cleanest automated gate-logic to borrow.
- **3(d) Sound/mechanical-block vs judgment-advise split:** still the most-validated choice. OpenSpec (mechanical CI) vs the advisory LLM layer remains the cleanest precedent.

## Updated reference-design pointer

For a single artifact hitting all four of your axes (three-valued + abstain, mandatory evidence, gating, separate critic): **firdasafridi/rfc-reviewer** remains the closest complete match, now joined by **wan-huiyan/agent-review-panel** (richest verdict+abstain shape) and **michaelhazza** (implements your exact drop-if-uncited rule). The most *adopted* real-world blocking gate is still **Spec Kit `/analyze`**, but it's a soft CRITICAL gate ("User may proceed"), not fail-closed.

**Net:** every one of your three differentiators (AMBIGUOUS verdict, cite-or-omit, fail-closed-on-mechanical/advisory-on-judgment) is now *better* supported than my first pass concluded — the abstain verdict and cite-or-omit are corroborated by multiple shipping 2026 tools, not just research papers. The only genuinely contrarian bet you're making is **fail-closed enforcement at claim-time** (vs advise/loop/human-approve), and qrspi shows even that has a precedent when coupled to mandatory citation.
